reviews.review_description: I’m not sure what Epic did to Harmonix after buying them… but it doesn’t look good. 😕

reviews.review_title: Why was it delisted from the store?
reviews.review_description: One of the best VR rhythm games, why was it delisted?

reviews.review_title: A must-buy for classic rhythm gamers
reviews.review_description: Great controls, good difficulty curve, high skill ceiling, easy to mod for custom songs, and cross buy, all from the pioneers of modern rhythm games. Right in the vein of Frequency, Amplitude, and Guitar Hero. A must buy for both Quest 2 users and PCVR users.
It's a good idea but the music sucks so much I want a refund

If you enjoyed rhythm games like Rockband or Guitar Hero this game might be worth a look for you. And If you like electronic dance music this game is even a must have! 😜
The controls are very intuitive. Easy to learn, hard to master. It’s very fluid, the soundtrack selection is a BLAST and the gameplay is very very addictive you really always want to get that personal Highscore and beyond! 😁
One of the most fun experiences I had so far in VR. Sometimes on higher difficulties you even feel like dancing even though you are „only“ shooting with guns. 🤣

It will suck you in at the lower levels and then it’s on!  If you love rhythm games and want to feel like a killer sharpshooter, this one is for you. It’s easy to learn the basics but hard to master as all the great games are. Pros: it has a huge variety of music available without needing to side load and a multilevel campaign mode that allows people newer to rhythm games to have a sense of completion. Also an achievement tracker which is nice for trophy chasers. Cons: some of the modifiers don’t come with any explanation and the no look targets are annoying with the heavy quest, can feel like whiplash at times. All in all it’s a fantastic companion to the legendary Beat Saber, as another reviewer said it’s the Han Solo to BS’s Luke Skywalker or like it starts to feel at the higher levels.., John Wick in space :)
I was on the fence because I owned beat saber and pistol whip but this is the best one and you have to play it to understand. Also if you are debating it just buy it i promise you will like it😃

reviews.review_title: Definitely a must have rhythm shooter!!!
reviews.review_description:
It’s like Guitar hero with Guns and moving targets that explode into beautiful fireworks when you hit them. Sometimes your guns shoot lasers some they shoot beautiful chains of lightning that you can swing around!
Using the thumb sticks you can even twirl your guns in your hands like a cowboy and throw them and have them return to you! Awesome game!!!
Only thing lacking is all the tracks are techno
NEEDS some rock and metal tunes/different genres would be AMAZING

Love it, it quickly became my favorite.   It keeps me engaged and I am learning to be in tune with the music.  It is a good way to connect the right and left hemispheres and get into a flow.  Love the challenge of trying to reach higher scores in order to climb the leaderboards, while taking in the beautiful firework show.

The action in this is purely pointing and shooting on beat, swinging at virtual blocks is more satisfying to me. However, unlike other rhythm games on here, it comes with a good soundtrack and the visuals are dank. The game sounds also mix really well with the songs.

If you’re spending much time with a music game, you’re going to hear the soundtrack on repeat. Guitar Hero and Rock Band had great soundtracks, and Harmonix has once again curated a game with songs I don’t mind hearing a few times. They figured out that popular electronic artists generally make better music than game designers messing around in Abelton, although the Dragonforce song is unforgivable and I don’t know why it’s a remix of Donna Summer’s “I Feel Love” when the original is perfect. 

The difficulty really ramps up at higher levels, I didn’t enjoy the challenges in the campaign like no look shots or not moving my hands, but it’s fun trying to climb the leaderboards for every song. It’s playable sitting down and working up a sweat is very optional. 

If you want a music game that comes with some good music, give this one a try.

This is possibly one of my favorite games on the quest store. By default, the aiming assist is turned all the way up which makes this feel more like an arcade rhythm game than a shooter game, however that feature can be adjusted and turned off entirely adding a whole new layer of challenge. With the aiming assist on, I can easily just point and shoot while dancing along to the great soundtrack, so don't worry if you're not a sharp shooter. 

Small tip, keep your eyes on the center dots that appear and try to shoot them on the beat(or the moment the target meets the dot) - looking at the targets as they move toward the dot will only throw you off.

Its kind of like pistol whip meets beat saber. If you wish pistol whip went to the music more, and beat saber made you feel cooler... That's this game. Just feel cool while shooting to great music. Most of the tracks are amazing with the flow and beat of the game. It just gives you a great feeling when you play. It takes about three songs to get the feel for the timing, but once you do it gets awesome.

I think this game has overall more polish and charm than Beatsaber and it's possibly even more addictive. The track list that comes with the game has more interesting variety and there are some really cool modern songs in paid DLC section. I hope they add more. Please support this game! It deserves to be a success.
This shooter game is cool for far more reasons than just for the sake of shooting objects. The timing, placement of shots and rhythm all count!!!
Really good game just missing something.

Having just bought it on sale and played about 5 songs, it does seem a bit repetitive and static.
The background environment doesn't change, you have to unlock other backgrounds.
I was hoping something like Pistol Whip or Air Trip, the player might get to move thru an environment or dodge objects but no its stationary target shooting.
One good thing is I can sit down while playing, for us broken old people, that's a bonus.
Good selection of songs but as they are all fast-tempo style it could have been any random song playing, I wouldn't have noticed any difference.

reviews.review_title: Bought it twice to show support
reviews.review_description:
I love how custom songs are possible without modding, even on the Quest, and how you get cross-play between the Quest and Rift for free. Beat Games, on the other hand did the bare minimum with their port to the Quest.
The gameplay is great, the little details like the rumble in the headset and controllers and the music getting clearer when your multiplier goes up are fantastic. It gives me goosebumps.
The selection of music is also fantastic, and while the quantity of free songs just about matches up with Beat Saber, the quality is much higher.

I read a bunch of reviews and after playing it for 5 minutes, i was hooked!!

I purchased the DLC packs and this game is amazing. 

If you are on the fence, get off it and start grooving. 

So much FUN!!
Only compliant would be I wish there were more songs to buy other then the 20 some 2019...

reviews.review_title: Absolute best VR rhythm game available!!
reviews.review_description: I’ve sunk dozens of hours into BS and PW, but Audica takes it to another level. One game is drum hero, the other is John Wick to a beat, but Audica does something neither one can: combine dancing with making you really feel you are conducting the music. I know it sounds crazy, but 3 songs in and I knew this was something special.  And there are a ton of songs, even without downloading the free ones. Haven’t even tried the pay to play songs, because I’m having too much fun with the base game. The campaign is excellently paced, ramping up song by song and really teaching you while you play.  And when you hold a beat, with both arms in the sky, waving them around to alter the sound, you really feel like a DJ in front of a crowd, about to drop a massive beat on them. Truly remarkable gameplay, but I shouldn’t be surprised coming from the guys that made Rock Band. I feel this game is just as revolutionary for VR rhythm games as Rock Band was for peripheral based music games. A must buy!!
